name: Nw Priority
description_short: NW Priority Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative serving since 1928, offering competitive loan rates, deposit accounts, and digital banking with a focus on personalized member service.
description_long:
NW Priority Credit Union (NWPCU) was founded in 1928 and has operated as a not-for-profit, member-owned financial cooperative for over 95 years. The credit union is federally insured through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund (NCUSIF), protecting member accounts up to at least $250,000 per individual. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, NWPCU operates on a people-over-profit model, returning earnings to members through better loan rates and higher deposit rates.

NWPCU offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, youth and teen accounts, term-shares, and money market accounts. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (as low as 4.49% APR), recreational vehicle loans, mortgage loans, personal loans (as low as 9.99% APR), and home equity lines of credit (HELOC) with promotional rates. Members also have access to Visa credit cards (Platinum card at 12.90% APR), financial planning services, and TruStage Insurance products.

NWPCU distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personal service and community involvement. The credit union highlights personalized member relationships, with staff familiarity mentioned in member testimonials. They offer modern digital banking including online banking, mobile banking with mobile deposit, bill pay, direct deposit, Zelle transfers, and wire transfers. The organization also provides an auto solution service positioning themselves as having a personal car shopper to help members find vehicles. Their membership model means members are stakeholders rather than customers, with voting rights and profit-sharing benefits.

As a traditional credit union with competitive rates and established infrastructure, NWPCU is best suited for consumers seeking conventional banking services with the cooperative ownership model. However, they are a regional credit union with specific membership eligibility requirements not detailed on their homepage, and potential members should verify they qualify to join. The credit union lacks information about membership field of eligibility, account minimums, and fee structures on their public-facing website.
pros: Auto loans available at rates as low as 4.49% APR, significantly below national averages
Personal Visa Platinum credit card with no annual fee and competitive 12.90% APR rate
NCUSIF deposit insurance protection up to $250,000 per individual account
Full suite of digital banking services including mobile banking with mobile deposit capability
HELOC introductory rate of 5.99% APR for 12 months (limited time offer)
Member-owned cooperative structure returns profits through better rates and lower fees
Over 95 years of operational history and established community presence since 1928
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly disclosed on website; may have field of eligibility restrictions typical of credit unions
Limited information about account minimum balances, monthly fees, or overdraft policies on homepage
No transparent fee schedule or comparison tool available on main website pages
Regional credit union with likely limited branch network and geographic service area
No information about personal loan rates starting at 9.99% APR or qualification requirements
best_for: Members seeking auto financing with competitive rates under 5% APR
Consumers who prioritize personalized, relationship-based banking over large institutional service
Individuals with $250,000+ in savings seeking credit union deposit insurance protection
Members wanting no-annual-fee credit card rewards for travel and everyday purchases
diagnosis: NW Priority Credit Union is best for consumers already eligible for membership who want competitive lending rates, member-owned cooperative benefits, and personalized banking service. The primary caveat is that credit union membership typically requires meeting specific eligibility criteria (field of eligibility, employer group, location, or family relationship) that are not detailed on their public website, so prospective members must verify they qualify before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Nw Priority — Credit Union in Portland, OR | CreditDoc
meta_description: Nw Priority — NCUA-insured credit union in Portland, OR. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
